items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork approved for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with the Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a Seeds of Peace Monument artwork by artist Mario Chiodo to be installed at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument memorializes survivors and reflects the city's commitment to peace and remembrance in a prominent public space.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set for 2026-27
items.summary: The council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget and annual assessment, determining funding levels for the district's operations and improvements in that area.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety section of General Plan
items.summary: The city council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan (covering 2015-2040). This element addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety policies that guide city development and operations.

items.headline: City Council appoints new permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This follows the staff contact listing Christian M. Curtis and marks a formal leadership appointment for the city's top administrative position.

items.headline: Duplex project permitted on Old Piedmont Road
items.summary: A new two-story duplex with 2 units receives a building permit at 2174 Old Piedmont Rd, representing a modest increase in residential density. At over $1 million, this is the largest single housing project in the recent permit batch.

items.headline: City releases draft urban water master plan and shortage contingency strategy
items.summary: Sunnyvale's Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plan addresses how the city will manage water supply and respond to shortages, a critical issue for South Bay residents facing ongoing drought and water availability challenges.

items.headline: Spring community grants and neighborhood event funding decisions announced
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications from the spring cycle on May 18. This grants program funds local community events and neighborhood initiatives, with awards determined through a competitive application process.

items.headline: Council to consider electrification program principles for air quality compliance
items.summary: Palo Alto is developing proposed principles for residential electrification programs to help the city meet South Bay Air District regulations. The council will hear a status update on the city's residential electrification efforts.
